topic: A person using a 'Self-Checkout' machine at a grocery store.

parsed_generation.script:
Scene 1 — Opening (0-2s): Side view of a lone shopper at a Self-Checkout kiosk; hand slides item toward the barcode scanner.
Scene 2 — Action (2-5s): Front view of the Self-Checkout touchscreen; finger taps to confirm; item rests in the bagging area.
Scene 3 — Close (5-8s): Over-the-shoulder tilt to the receipt printer; shopper grabs receipt and steps away; screen shows completed transaction.
parsed_generation.narration: A lone shopper uses a Self-Checkout machine, charting a quick, low-contact path to purchase. The cut emphasizes the tactile feedback of the screen and the scanner as key interfaces. The scene hints at efficiency and privacy benefits in busy grocery aisles.

feedback: The sequence matches the topic and uses action-first prompts, but visuals are fairly minimal. Increase visual richness with varied angles, on-screen details (scan line, bagging area), and subtle ambience. Tighten narration to stay concrete and observable, and consider adding a brief goal or outcome at the end.
strengths: Focused on a single subject and device
fixes: Add more camera angles and sensory detail (sound, lighting)
Provide more concrete, observable narration aligned with visuals
